statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find all pairs of subarrays whose sums are equal. The subarrays can be of any length and can be non-overlapping. For example, given the array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], one possible pair of subarrays is [1, 2, 3] and [4, 5]. Write a function that returns all pairs of subarrays whose sums are equal.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a list of pairs of subarrays whose sums are equal.
statement.notes: The sums of the subarrays should be equal, but the subarrays themselves do not have to be equal.

constraints.description: The length of the input array.
The target sum of the subarrays.

examples.explanation: One possible pair of subarrays is [1, 2, 3] and [4, 5].
Two possible pairs of subarrays are [10, 20] and [30, 40], and [10, 30] and [20, 40].

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to find all possible subarray sums.

editorial.title: Efficient Subarray Sum Finder
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use dynamic programming to find all possible subarray sums and then compare them to find pairs of subarrays with equal sums.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to generate all possible subarrays and then check each pair to see if their sums are equal.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use dynamic programming to find all possible subarray sums in O(n^2) time. Then, we can compare these sums to find pairs of subarrays with equal sums in O(n^2) time.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a hashmap to store the subarray sums and their indices. Then, we can iterate through the hashmap and compare each pair of subarray sums to find pairs with equal sums.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that it correctly finds all pairs of subarrays with equal sums.
editorial.dryRun: Here is a dry run of the solution:
commonMistakes: Not using a hashmap to store subarray sums.

functionSignature.java: public List<List<Integer>> findPairs(int[] nums, int sum);
